Something just dropped that most builders haven't noticed yet. @subquadratic shipped the world's first commercial subquadratic LLM. 12 million token context window. nearly 1,000× less compute than transformers. ~1/20th the cost of Claude Opus on long-context tasks. For 8 years, every major AI system, GPT, Claude, and Gemini has been built on the same transformer math from 2017. SubQ just broke that ceiling. And most people are still arguing about which chatbot is smarter. and most people are still arguing about which chatbot is smarter. #ai #subq #llm #buildinpublic #aiagents

Yesterday I published a short video explaining why we are in an AI infrastructure bubble. This would materially decrease memory needed for constant demand and impact $MU. @AppenResearch has done an initial validation of their results. The key sentence is "The result is an architecture that scales linearly with context length instead of quadratically." We are still pending further validations. @AppenResearch independently evaluated @subquadratic's SSA kernel - a learned sparse attention mechanism designed to reduce the quadratic scaling limitations of full attention. Results at 1M-token context lengths: - 56.2× wall clock speedup vs. FA2 - 62.8× FLOP reduction (validated via torch.profiler, <4% variance from theoretical) - 95.6% average score across RULER tasks at 128K - 86.2% average score on the hardest MRCR 8-needle bucket (512K–1M contexts) - 81.8% SWE-Bench Verified resolved rate Full report: https://t.co/9OpvaqDPbt

The cost to run GPT-3.5 level intelligence fell 280x in two years. On PhD-level reasoning tasks, inference costs are dropping 40x per year and now @subquadratic’ 52x cheaper long-context on top of that. Every major platform shift followed a compute cost collapse. + Cheap bandwidth gave us streaming + Cheap mobile compute gave us Instagram AI inference is collapsing faster than either of those curves. The apps that win next are the ones that use the most intelligence per dollar, not the least.

AI’s biggest bottleneck was never intelligence. It was memory. Every AI company built an entire infrastructure stack around one limitation: LLMs cannot actually remember very much at once. 🤯 That is how AI memory became a workaround economy. @subquadratic's bet is that the fix is not better duct tape. It is an architecture that can actually read millions of tokens at once. Today, the startup launched SubQ: ⚡ A 12M-token LLM ⚡ 1/5 the cost of frontier models ⚡ 52× faster than FlashAttention at 1M tokens ⚡ Better long-context retrieval scores than GPT-5.4, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and Claude Opus 4.7 in MRCR v2

Who > What. @suno just announced a well-deserved funding round at a $5.4B valuation. I was fortunate to be their sole pre-seed investor, but I have to confess, I didn’t love the music business when we invested back in 2022. At the time, crypto was king and AI was still a novelty. Music apps are a notoriously challenging category. ChatGPT hadn’t yet landed in the hands of users and showed what was possible. But this team marched to the beat of their own drum. When I met @MikeyShulman, Martin and Georg, they were a trio of wizards who understood audio and AI better than anyone on the planet. I had misgivings about the music business, but had total conviction about the team. Suno wasn’t actively fundraising, so I pitched @fcollective. Luckily Mikey thought we may be able to add some value ... It was well worth the effort. Markets evolve, products pivot, and technologies come in and out of fashion, but brilliant, authentic, agentic founders are the constant around which great startups are built. Invest in the "who,” trust that the “what” will follow.

Today, I walked past the Old Street roundabout on my way to a SXSW London side event, and saw @synthesiaIO on a billboard with some of the best companies Europe has ever produced. I'll admit it gave me a much-needed energy boost ahead of speaking on yet another panel about AI's problems. The billboard is part of #BuiltinEurope, a campaign @balderton has launched to tell the truth about what is happening right now in European tech. The story we've heard for years has been one of catching up. Europe could lead, the headlines said, if only it had fixed this or that. That's because building in Europe was treated as a thing you had to overcome. Now, for the first time in a long while, the opposite is true. Met with indifference at best and opposition at worst at home, European founders are forced to build companies that work everywhere from the first day, and they're doing it with style (and substance). At Synthesia we feel this in our bones. We were built in London and we have been global from the start, because being European made that the only sensible way to think. So if you have ever thought about building a company in Europe, or joining one, consider this your invitation. https://t.co/cvFiwU9dG3 is now live, with a jobs platform pulling together open roles from a thousand European startups, and a directory of the continent's best incubators for anyone ready to start something of their own. Europe is building. Come build with us.

To be clear, @synthesiaIO is proud to be part of Built in Europe and the case @balderton makes is the right one. I am less comfortable when that ambition gets folded or confused with the version of sovereignty taking shape in Brussels, where the instinct is to reduce reliance on foreign technology through procurement rules, subsidies, and a thickening layer of complex and overlapping regulation. Real sovereignty comes from building generational companies strong enough to win on open ground. Shielding them from competition only swaps one dependence for another and creates inferior products and services at higher costs. We need more of the former, and less of the latter.

Today, @synthesiaIO's security team is announcing a new multi-agent system that allows us to keep pace with the high volume of AI-generated code shipping through our codebase: https://t.co/8prHK0qa9D As more companies use AI to write their software, they will inadvertently create security flaws which can then be exploited by bad faith hackers equipped with powerful AI models. Claude Mythos is expected to help with this issue, but the model is currently available only to select Anthropic partners, not the general public. Therefore, we are sharing the recipe for our tool so we can help any company approach Mythos-levels of security performance today, with off-the-shelf models that are generally available. Additionally, our recipe achieves this performance at a reduced cost, meaning it can be used by larger and smaller companies alike. @mtschammer, head of security at Synthesia, spoke with @natrubio__ from @theDeepView about the design decisions we've made and how this project came to life: https://t.co/TvhbwiYbqW

Our head of security @mtschammer was at VantaCon today, talking about how @synthesiaIO uses AI to make our own security function better, not just the product we sell. The clip below is from a conversation he recorded with @lukeknight and @Ronanchamberss from @etnshow right before walking on stage. The short version: enterprise customers send us a lot of detailed security questions, and rightly so. We've trained an agent on our security documentation that handles a meaningful share of that work on our @TrustVanta-powered trust portal, which gives the security team room to focus on the harder problems that actually need a human in the loop. My main takeaway from their chat was this: a lot of the conversation on AI security is usually framed as a tradeoff. More AI means more risk to manage. What Martin is describing is closer to the opposite: the same technology we use to build for our customers is making the people who protect them more effective at their jobs. For example, we've built a code review process that achieves Mythos-levels of security performance, at a fraction of the cost. We'll share more on this topic soon!

SOP driven problem solving for using LLM tools like @windsurf_ai @cursor_ai @tabnine - based on 350+ hours of playing with them over the last few months - Create a new folder for context retention docs called aa_ref_01 - Create file named r01_initial_ideation.txt for just noting down all the ideas without any structure - Create file named r02_domain_analysis.txt which will contain the best of the conversation we have with tab nine or @web in order summarize the best domain knowledge needed for the problem statement - Create file named r03_prd_v1.txt which will be the first PRD for the user journey at a high level - Create file named r04_prd_v2.txt which hopefully is a more precise version of the previous PRD based on multiple iterations on the previous file - Create file named r05_arch_L1.txt which will contain the first attempt at the architecture of the PRD - this file should leverage a relationship of consistency with r04_prd_v2.txt + r02_domain_analysis.txt PAUSE HERE & NOTICE: a. LLMs should be treated as search + assimilation tools, you have to bias them with right keywords b. LLMs need to iterate to arrive at a more refined output c. LLMs need to sumarize information which will lost by the sliding context window, hence you constantly create new docs which are in sync with previous docs now - Create file named r06_arch_L2.txt which will contain the second iteration of the architecture of the PRD - this file should leverage a relationship of consistency with r05_arch_L1.txt + r04_prd_v2.txt + r02_domain_analysis.txt - Create file named r07_explain_architecture.txt which will contain the explanation of the architecture of the PRD - this file should leverage a relationship of consistency with r06_arch_L2.txt + r04_prd_v2.txt + r02_domain_analysis.txt. This file is basically a rubber-duck debugging of the architecture with us as the listener who wants to make sure that the r06_arch_L2.txt is well thought out - Create a file named r08_TDD_v1.txt which will basically ask the LLM to write a TDD plan to implement the r06_arch_L2.txt in the most minimalistic way possible, it should be consistent with r04_prd_v2.txt & r02_domain_analysis.txt - If the r08_TDD_v1.txt is good enough, then select each step of the TDD plan as a checkbox - and ask the strong LLM (code LLM) to implement the TDD plan in the most idiomatic bug-free way possible line by line, thoroughly following the TDD methodology of starting with Red Test, then Green Test, then Refactor - Create a file named r09_bug_log.txt to note copy all the bugs encountered in the process of implementing the TDD plan - Create a file named r10_avoid_bugs.txt which will contain meta patterns and best practices discovered during the implementation of the TDD plan - Create UAT plan for the whole feature, ideally this should be very close to r04_prd_v2.txt - and manually check the results, and document them in r11_uat_results.txt - Create a file named r99_unclassified.txt to note down all the prompts, ideas, mistakes, lack of knowledge or references or links which you encounter during the journey with timestamps PAUSE HERE & NOTICE: d. LLMs need to be asked to reason themselves so they can find their own logical fallacies, like rubber duck debugging e. LLMs need to be given reference files for patterns to avoid, be it via past bugs or anti patterns to avoid f. LLMs need to follow TDD because it makes it easier to arrive at the final code - it is an easier prediction problem if you know what will be the requisite final pattern, also helps you avoid mistakes early enough g. LLM whispering is a cumulative skill so constantly document your prompts, steps & learnings p.s. most won't be able to follow the above steps and would rather smash "fix this" every 2 minutes because they lack the patience to follow these steps methodically

TripoSplat from @tripoai a lightweight image-to-3D Gaussian model for fast, controllable 3D asset generation. 🔗https://t.co/EkeN1JlWJ9 ✅ Adjustable Gaussian count up to 262,144 ✅ Two core files, ~2,000 LOC total, for lightweight implementation. ✅ Near-zero dependencies, no transformers or diffusers required. ✅ Official ComfyUI support Released under the MIT License for flexible commercial and non-commercial use. For image-to-3D and Gaussian Splatting workflows, TripoSplat offers a practical way to reduce setup cost while keeping quality and rendering cost controllable. 📚Paper：https://t.co/Vgzb4nrzI8

Mine is 30 min ea for more of a hero asset look, custom splat count, custom baked lighting, full spherical harmonics.. Tripo is actually quite fast, no harmonics, and fairly blurry texture with some stronger hallucinations on geo.. Can be a really nice tool for background scene dressings and if you're using something like DLSS5 in the future maybe it's perfectly poised to enhance, BUT if you don't mind 30 min and have a 5090+ my Image2Splat is pretty awesome!

VoiceMoat vs Typefully A beautiful editor won't fix a generic voice. @Typefully is the best thread composer in the AI Twitter category. Not "good for an AI tool." Good by any standard. Keyboard shortcuts, thread blocks, split view, multi-platform publishing. @VoiceMoat is a voice model trained on your actual posts. Auden learns your 9 voice dimensions, generates drafts in your specific style, scores every output 0-100 against your profile. Both tools live in the X creator stack. Both have serious users. Both produce publishable content. Which is why the comparison keeps coming up. They solve different bottlenecks. A creator with Typefully but no voice model has a beautiful editor producing generic-AI drafts. The writing surface is great. The output sounds like everyone else using AI on X. A creator with VoiceMoat but no thread composer has voice-accurate AI in a functional interface. The output sounds like them. The composing experience is fine but not elegant. The right answer for most serious creators is both. When budget only allows one, the question is which problem is louder right now: If the writing surface itself is slow and fragmented.. or you publish to 5 platforms simultaneously.. or your voice is already strong and AI tools already sound like you.. Typefully. If AI drafts feel generic and your audience is giving you "did you use AI?" signals.. or you live in replies and need in-feed drafting.. or you want to measure voice drift over time.. VoiceMoat. The editor question and the voice question are separate. Know which one you're actually hitting.

Distribution is king, and that’s never been more true for content creators. If you’re a creator using @typefully and @claudeai, you’re leaving a lot on the table if you’re not using this 3-step method to optimize your posts: - Set up your Typefully MPC in Claude - Ask Claude to run an analytical review of your post using the prompt below - Review and take action as per suggestion --- You are an expert Social Media Strategist and Data Analyst. Objective: Perform a comprehensive performance audit of my [Platform Name, e.g., X, LinkedIn, Threads] account using my exported analytics data from Typefully MCP. Provide a strategy that balances growth with time-efficiency. → Content DNA & ROI The “Stickiness” Factor: Identify which content categories (e.g., Educational, Personal Stories, News Updates, etc.) have the highest Save/Bookmark-to-Reach ratio. Which posts are people keeping for later vs. just scrolling past? The “Show More” Trigger: For long-form text or threads, analyze the hook (the first 2 lines). Which opening styles consistently get users to click and read the full post? Media vs. Message: Compare the performance of text-only posts vs. posts with images/video. Is the extra effort of creating visuals resulting in a proportional increase in Profile Visits or Link Clicks? → Time & Velocity Mapping The High-Engagement Window: Pinpoint the 60-minute windows when my audience is most “reactive.” Don’t just give me a time—explain the Engagement Velocity (how fast interactions happen in the first 30 minutes) and how it affects overall reach. The “Quiet Hour” Opportunity: Are there times when engagement is lower but reach is high? Identify whether I should post during off-peak hours to face less competition in the feed. → Repost/Retweet Spacing Strategy The Frequency Audit: I currently repost my own content at [Insert your intervals, e.g., 1hr - 6hr - 12hr]. Based on the data, is this causing “feed fatigue” (the same people seeing the post too many times), or is it successfully reaching new time zones? The 24-Hour Flywheel: Design an optimized repost schedule that covers three distinct global regions (e.g., morning in the US, EU, and Asia) without overlapping on a single user’s timeline more than once every 8 hours. → Conversion & Growth Funnel Follower Attribution: Which specific posts actually drove New Follows? Distinguish between "Viral Hits" (high views/low follows) and "Community Builders" (low views/high follows). Bio & Call-to-Action (CTA) Check: Based on the spikes in profile visits, suggest 3 ways to optimize my Bio or Pinned Post to convert more visitors into long-term followers. ---

A vibe coding platform I’m really starting to like @v0 now doesn't just generate the interface. It checks it in the browser itself For prototypes, landing pages, dashboards, forms, MVPs and internal tools, this is a very good update The old flow was always a bit annoying. From the outside everything looks kinda beautiful, then you open preview and: > button doesn't work > form shifts > mobile version looks weird Now v0 acts more like an agent than a simple UI generator. It has Browser use, so it can open the app it just built, check scenarios, look at the design through the browser and try to fix issues before you even start poking around manually. The best part is that it shows screenshots from these browser checks. So you see what the agent opened, what it saw and at what step it checked the result. Also v0 has an official iOS app. > Old loop was generate, open, find a bug, write a fix, check again > New loop is generate, agent opens it, spots some issues on its own, fixes them and shows screenshots

@rauchg I built something and I think you should see it. It started with a simple frustration. Every time a team outgrows @vercel pricing, their only option is to self-host and self-hosting means rebuilding everything from scratch and losing all the DX they loved. That felt like a solvable problem. So I built BYOC. You just click connect AWS button then cloudFormation runs inside your own account and sets everything up. My platform never touches your data. But then I thought why stop at AWS? What if someone wants GCP next year? So I built a provider abstraction layer. AWS is live right now. But the platform has no opinion on which cloud you use. Swap it out, everything else stays exactly the same. Took me 4 weeks solo. Let's build @vercel in a way that user actually want. Here's the full breakdown 👇

A pattern we've noticed on Show Me Your Stack: The best builders have a system for catching ideas before they disappear. @vercel founder, @rauchg showed @julesrosenberg Timeless, an internal tool he uses to capture bugs, product feedback, screenshots, screen recordings, and quick thoughts while he's in the middle of working. A few use cases he shared: > recording bugs the moment they happen > capturing UI feedback without writing a long ticket > saving product ideas before they disappear > sharing context with teammates asynchronously > creating a running backlog from real-world usage The common thread: less time documenting, more time noticing. What's interesting is how much of product development comes down to collecting observations before they're forgotten.

🔒 Fragmented identity pipelines are failing against digital injection attacks. .@Veriff is the identity layer for the internet: matching people to government-issued IDs with a selfie so companies can verify users instantly and stop fraud at global scale. What began in Estonia has grown into one of the world’s leading identity verification systems, processing hundreds of millions of checks and analyzing thousands of signals per user. Founder @kaarelkotkas joined YC’s @gustaf to share how he went from a high-school prototype to serving global fintechs and marketplaces, why identity is an infrastructure problem, and why he sees a future where your digital passport might be issued not by governments — but by Veriff. 00:00 – How Veriff Started in a High School Computer Lab 01:52 – Why Online Identity Is an Infrastructure Problem 04:58 – Matching IDs With a Selfie 07:10 – Fighting Fraud in Fintech & Marketplaces 10:53 – Scaling Verification to a Global Platform 15:44 – YC, Early Breakthroughs, and Hard Lessons 20:31 – Deepfakes, Signals, and the New Threat Landscape 26:33 – Building a Mission-Driven Team 32:48 – The Digital Passport Vision

Fading Through Beautiful Versions of Her Created with Seedance 2.0 on @ViduAI_official Prompt Title: Mirror Portal Dressing Room A hyper-stylish cinematic fashion scene inside a dim, futuristic dressing room filled with glowing mirrors and soft neon reflections. A single elegant female model stands in front of a tall mirror framed with subtle luminous edges. The atmosphere is quiet, dreamy, and surreal—floating dust particles in light beams, soft ambient hum. 0–3s: She slowly approaches the mirror. Her reflection is slightly delayed, almost alive. 3–6s: She gently touches the mirror surface. Ripples of light spread like liquid glass. 6–10s (Transformation Sequence): Each touch triggers a rapid aesthetic shift: • Vintage 70s outfit with warm film grain tones • Y2K glossy streetwear with metallic textures • Futuristic chrome bodysuit with holographic glow • Royal couture gown with gold embroidery and floating fabric physics The mirror acts like a portal—each outfit briefly “pulls through” from the reflection into reality. 10–13s: The camera orbits around her as the transformations sync with her movement. Lighting constantly reshapes the scene. 13–15s (Final Moment): She smiles slightly, resting her hand on the mirror. The surface stabilizes, showing all four versions of her reflected at once in split ethereal layers. Fade out with soft glow. Style keywords (add to generator): cinematic fashion film, surreal mirror portal, luxury aesthetic, soft neon lighting, high-detail fabric simulation, shallow depth of field, 4K, ultra-real, dreamy atmosphere, slow motion transformation. #vidu

Aave's stablecoin is more serious than you think. T-bills are at 3.71%. @SkyEcosystem 's sUSDS sits at 3.6%. GHO targets a minimum 50bps above it, currently paying 4.25%. The yield comes from borrowers paying interest to mint GHO; @DefiLlama shows $5.1M in annualized revenue from that alone. Only 76% of the GHO supply is staked to earn that yield (per @TokenLogic). With fewer recipients sharing the same rewards pool, the effective rate increases. If GHO ever falls short of that 50bps premium, @aave generates $125M/year in protocol revenue and can cover the difference. >Total GHO supply: $212M > Old stkGHO (paying less now): $77M of that Btw, if you still hold old stkGHO, you need to migrate. I think they can scale this without touching treasury at all. More GHO circulating means the 76% staking rate could drop even lower, keeping individual yields high without Aave spending anything. A stablecoin backed by a $125M revenue machine doesn't easily lose the rate war. I'll go deeper into the table attached if you want to 😜

感谢大家前段时间的支持，今天@remio_ai 发布2.2版本，同时开始商业化。希望大家理解和支持，让remio能够建立健康的商业模式，能够永远为大家服务下去。 remio也会提供免费版，虽然免费版的积分肯定是比较有限的，但对于偶尔需要查找资料或者一个月做做二三十个小时的会议纪要应该也够用。

周末不到两天时间指挥AI写了差不多2000行代码了，预研了两个功能：Apple Notes笔记内容实时同步与解析、Excel文档的解析和问答。 Apple Notes同步和解析基本已经做完，搞定了最复杂的表格内容解析和附件的提取，不久将会集成到 @remio_ai 里。 Notes的表格数据的表示非常复杂，如果没有AI，不是说要多久才能实现的问题，是觉得不知道能不能实现压根就不会去干的问题。Claude Sonnet-4.5尝试了多次，始终没有解决行列乱序问题，换成GPT-5才终于解决。但附件提取GPT-5又没搞定，换成Sonnet搞定了。 Excel文档问答会更难，现有的方案虽然初步能走通，但纯靠大模型暴力回答。大模型连数数都数不清楚，还不实用。实用的方案最好是大模型、Text2SQL、Python脚本三管齐下。这方面还需要更多的实验。 用AI编程，就像建筑师在指挥施工队，施工队帮你做了很多体力活，但整个建筑还是要你设计，施工过程也需要经常把关。

这几天因为做把Apple Notes的数据接入到@remio_ai的功能，又笔记密集的体验了下最新的AI coding。 我的感觉，AI写代码很厉害，不能说代码写的多好，但也不能说烂，关键是快而且对主要的库和函数贼熟。 但感觉AI做设计还是相当的不靠谱。可能我对设计的要求比较高，希望简洁明了，不要过度设计，该复用的时候要复用。感觉AI现在还远远做不到，本来几十行就能实现的需求，它经常搞出几百行来，冗余的重复的设计不少。 腾讯据说50%代码是AI生成的了？可能这50%里也有大量的过度设计吧。

今天OpenAI推出了自己的AI浏览器ChatGPT Atlas，显示了其过剩的资源和无处安放的精力。ChatGPT Atlas也没什么创新，约等于Dia加@remio_ai 1.0的web auto capture。 ChatGPT Atlas的Browser Memories “致敬”了remio早在3月初推出时首创的自动保存网页内容的思路，说明这个思路开始被业界认可，再次证明我们走的路是对的。 我们在七个半月前的1.0版本就提供了Browser Memories，现在我们都已经升级到2.0了。OpenAI，你落后太多啦。 而且我们认为这是用户的数据，应该归用户，所以这些数据我们都存在用户电脑本地，而不是像OpenAI一样把数据放在云上，还不能导出。 数据属于用户（及所在的企业），应该能够自由的用任何最合适的大模型来处理。用户拥有这个权利，任何把用户数据强行跟模型绑定的行为，只可能来自于资本的利益而非用户利益。 OpenAI就是因为从资本的利益出发，想要用户数据，所以Browser Memories默认是不敢打开的。

Quick comments on ChatGPT Atlas Today, OpenAI launched its own AI browser, ChatGPT Atlas, which highlights its abundant resources and restless energy. However, ChatGPT Atlas shows little innovation — it’s roughly equivalent to Dia plus @remio_ai 1.0. We introduced browser memories in remio seven months ago, and we believe that this data belongs to the user. Therefore, we store all of it locally on the user’s computer, rather than doing what OpenAI does — keeping the data on their cloud servers and not allowing users to export it. Data belongs to the user and should be freely processed with whichever large model is most suitable. Users have this right — any attempt to forcibly bind user data to a specific model can only stem from capital interests, not user interests.

自3月初发布内测版以来，经过7个月紧锣密鼓的开发，今天我们正式发布 @remio_ai 2.0版本。 3月初发布内测版时，很多人以为我们是一个笔记应用，因为首页就是一个Note列表，除了网页自动保存也不支持其他数据源，问答功能也很基础。 但这从来不是我们的定位。这7个月，我们极大增强了数据源整合能力和AI助手能力。 🧠 数据源整合，构建最全面的个人记忆 最受欢迎的是文件夹同步，再也不需要手动上传文件到ChatGPT； 网页自动保存不仅支持普通资讯网页，还能自动保存你和ChatGPT、Gemini、DeepSeek等AI的聊天记录、YouTube字幕、在线PDF、Google Docs、Twitter thread； 无限量免费录音转写——无论线上会议、现场研讨会还是线下讲座，remio都能录制、转写，并即时生成包含关键决策和行动项的AI摘要； 此外还支持导入Markdown笔记、同步Slack和邮件数据。 remio 2.0整合文件、网站、Slack、邮件等所有内容，打破信息孤岛，构建最全面的个人记忆。 🔒 local-first和BYOK提供100%的隐私保护 为此保驾护航的是local-first技术架构：所有数据在本地保存、解析和索引，只使用API调用LLM，确保数据不会用于LLM训练。你还可以使用自己的LLM key（BYOK），此时remio将完全不接触你的任何内容。 🤖 最前沿的AI助手能力 支持GPT、Gemini、Claude、DeepSeek等最前沿的LLM； 强大的意图理解引擎，听懂工作中的典型话术，精准搜索资料； 通过@针对当前文档、项目进行精准问答； 浏览器插件让问答能力触手可及； 精准到句子级别的引用来源，核实信息异常方便； Prompt Library让常见提示词方便复用。

赶在节点发布 @remio_ai v1.18版。从去年9月开始开发，到今天已经整整一年了，这个版本增加了联网搜索的功能，和个人知识库一起构成全面的上下文能力。从此remio不再只是懂你，还懂世界！ 作为一个问答产品，到今天才提供联网搜索是很少见的，主要是想测试一下纯个人知识库的问答是否有真需求。如果一开始就提供联网搜索问答，就可能搞不清楚。 到了今天，个人知识库问答的需求已经得到了验证，加上联网搜索能力也是很自然的事。这也是非常常见的需求。 这个版本还提供了试验性的GMail邮件同步功能，首期只有100个内测名额，先到先得。希望申请内测的可以DM我。 这个版本还显著提升了Ask的便利性和精准性。便利性方面，在内容卡片可以一键@提问，方便看到什么就针对性的问答。精准性方便，remio对时间范围的筛选更精准，并修复了有时引用信息错乱的顽固bug。

这两天微信占存储空间大的事情又在中推上闹的沸沸扬扬的。@remio_ai 考虑到保护用户的数据安全和隐私（并且remio也不是社交或协作软件，没有必要数据上云），和微信一样把数据存储在本地，空间占用的问题是我们从第一天就要认真考虑的。 当前的实现肯定远非完美，但remio的关键设计都充分考虑到减少空间占用的目标，比如： 1、不保存网页的原始格式内容，只保存网页正文内容的Markdown，不自动保存X首页等内容很多的信息流，这使得重度用户每个月网页自动保存的文本数据也不过20MB。 2、不保存网页的原始图片格式，都转成中等质量的webp格式，这使得重度用户每个月网页自动保存的图片数据也不过200MB级别。 3、精细的URL去重处理，避免同一个网页保存多次。很多网页带动态参数，有很多参数不影响网页内容（如推广的utm_source, referer之类），但很多参数又会影响内容（很多SaaS是这样），所以不能一刀切处理，这个方面我们持续优化策略，目前应该基本上不会重复保存了。 4、对用户已有的本地文件不增加一份存储（所有要把文件上云的产品都增加了一份存储），而只是对文件内容做了索引。初步看索引占用的空间只有原始文件的10%左右。 5、录音使用的是m4a格式，一个小时只有10MB出头的大小。 6、邮件（这个正在开发）也只保存邮件正文的TXT格式和附件的Markdown，不会导致邮件数据又被存了一份。 后续我们还会提供智能空间管理能力。网页图片和录音是相对比较吃空间的，后续我们将会提供一键清理几个月之前且没有访问过和被引用的图片和录音的功能，这样那些很久以前没用的数据就会被清掉，那些虽然很久了但还有用的数据就会被自动保留。 remio的深度用户把一辈子的记忆都交给remio了，remio要担得起用户的信任和期望。万一哪天remio真的活不下去了，remio帮你保存、解析、转录的那些数据，都以Markdown和图片的格式存储在你电脑上，你也随时可以导入到别的类似软件里去。

没想到宝玉 @dotey 和大辉 @Fenng 就微信编辑器的事居然吵起来了，我也说说我的看法。 大辉说编辑器看着简单其实不好做是没错的，我们当年做网易博客的时候就做过两代编辑器，后来兄弟部门做有道云笔记和灵犀文档（内部用的类似飞书）也有所参与，现在做@remio_ai 也要做编辑器，确实感觉不太好做。 我也是觉得微信公众号后台的编辑器很不好用，在这点上我和宝玉的体感很像。但这主要是因为我习惯Markdown风格的编辑器，是个人风格的原因。我也不太认同池大说的如果对排版的要求比较简单的话，微信的编辑器也OK，感觉不好用是因为对排版的要求花样多。我觉得是反的，我是觉得越简单越会觉得不好用，因为简单排版Markdown好用多了，但对排版要求高的话，微信编辑器配合插件的能力上限很高。别的编辑器搞不定的似乎微信都能搞定，因为开放了很强的裸HTML功能。 我个人觉得，微信编辑器做成这样是因为微信在设计时更注重能力上限和灵活性，这样可以让插件尽情发挥。但与此同时，也导致了官方编辑器本身提供的能力处在底层，有点毛胚房的感觉。这可能是无奈之举，如果官方编辑器自身的功能很强很丰富，就不免会带来一些结构性约束，导致插件都很难处理。 举个例子，很多插件都提供特殊的标题样式，如果官网编辑器支持Markdown，那你输入## 是不是应该自动变成插件的二级标题样式呢？这其实很不容易做到。 但是我也是不能理解为什么不支持直接粘贴图片而只能上传。以我的了解，支持粘贴图片应该没什么副作用。

ChatGPT以来一直有一种所有应用都值得用GenAI重做一遍的观点。我在最早的时候也曾经这么想，但很快就觉得可能不对，但主要是一种直觉，说不出来为啥可能不对。 但在这一年做 @remio_ai 的过程中，开始逐步体会到，GenAI应用和已有应用可能更多的是增强和共生的关系，而不是替代关系。 比如因为remio支持文件夹同步，所以就有用户把remio和Obsidian合着用，记笔记还是在Obsidian，但用remio来问答。再比如不少用户有希望把Apple Notes和Notion的数据接入到remio的需求，这些用户也并不是想用remio代替Apple Notes和Notion。 新的能力和已有软件形成共生而非替代关系在历史上是有先例的，如数据仓库和BI。数据仓库和BI技术出现的时候，已经有不少软件了，数据仓库和BI做的是集成这些已有软件的数据并提供分析能力，并没有替代这些软件。 另一方面，历史上大面积的软件替代发生是有原因的，SaaS对OP软件、移动App对PC软件的大面积替代是因为计算平台发生的巨大的变化。不同计算平台之间共生很难，所以很多情况下只能重做。GenAI是一个很靠后的技术，用户所直接接触的计算平台没变，所以可能不容易带来大面积的重做和替代。 在前司的时候做了很多互联网应用，现在做AI应用，感觉很不一样。之前的互联网应用感觉都是独立的，想怎么做怎么做，但remio需要考虑非常多的和已有的操作系统和软件怎么连通、互操作，有非常多的约束条件。

🚨 @ResolvLabs USR just got exploited: here's the full on-chain breakdown h/t @yieldsandmore who flagged this first | data via @ArkhamIntel An attacker deposited 100K USDC into Resolv's USR Counter contract via requestSwap and received 49,950,000 USR back (~$39M) That's a 500× overcredit on a $100K deposit. The minting function is broken. On-chain receipts: → 100,000 USDC sent to Resolv: USR Counter (0xa27a...5861) → 50,000,000 USR minted from null address to Counter → 49,950,000 USR forwarded to attacker (0x04A288a7...caEd) → 100,000 USDC sent to intermediary (0xacB7027f...2b8e) The _targetAmount in the input data reads: 50,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000 (50M × 10^18) The requestSwap → completeSwap is a 2-step async process. Either the oracle was gamed, the off-chain signer was compromised, or the amount validation between request and completion is simply missing. The attacker's exit playbook is textbook DeFi hack cashout running at full speed: Step 1 — Wrap USR → wstUSR to access deeper DEX liquidity 20M USR → 17.65M wstUSR 15M USR → 13.24M wstUSR Step 2 — Dump wstUSR across every available venue 8.77M wstUSR → 9.7M USDT (KyberSwap) 2M wstUSR → 2.01M USDC (direct contract 0x04a2...caed) 1.31M wstUSR → 655K USDT (KyberSwap) 1.31M wstUSR → 148K USDT (KyberSwap — slippage getting brutal) 604K wstUSR → 568K USDT 300K wstUSR → 277K USDC (Velora) 300K wstUSR → 303K USDC (Velora) Dozens of 100K-150K wstUSR clips through Velora at varying slippage Step 3 — Convert stables → ETH aggressively 4.85M USDT → 2,297 ETH (contract 0xbeef...c555) 1.66M USDT → 789 ETH (Uniswap V4) 2.02M USDC → 948 ETH (MetaMask Swaps) 1.5M USDT → 703 ETH (MetaMask Swaps) 2M USDT → 938 ETH (MetaMask Swaps) 808K USDT → 384 ETH 760K USDT → 362 ETH 656K USDT → 312 ETH 370K USDT → 174 ETH Yes @MetaMask Swaps for multi-million dollar legs 😅 wstUSR selling at $0.50-$0.88 on the dollar across different trades, with slippage worsening as liquidity drains. Multiple failed transactions visible on-chain showing the urgency. Estimated total extraction: $25M+ and counting. The attacker is still actively dumping remaining wstUSR positions as of this post. For context Resolv had ~$500M+ TVL, an @immunefi bug bounty of $500K, Fireblocks custody integration, and multiple audits including a Sherlock competition. Audits ≠ security. Monitoring partnerships ≠ prevention. The core question: how did a 100K USDC requestSwap get authorized as a 50M USR completeSwap? Someone needs to explain what happened between those two steps. @PeckShieldAlert @peckshield @SlowMist_Team @hypaboreal you may want to take a look 👀

BTC: volumen agregado y volatilidad vuelven a coincidir En Bitcoin, los picos de volumen no siempre aparecen aislados. @santimentfeed En la gráfica se observa cómo el 6 de febrero el aumento del volumen agregado de BTC coincidió prácticamente con un repunte claro de la volatilidad semanal. En Santiment, esta métrica debe entenderse como una lectura agregada de actividad del mercado. No separa de forma directa cuánto corresponde a spot, derivados, OTC u otros movimientos on-chain, sino que actúa como un proxy amplio de actividad sobre BTC. Por eso, cuando el volumen agregado y la volatilidad se activan al mismo tiempo, el mercado suele estar mostrando una fase de mayor participación, mayor agresividad operativa y posible cambio de ritmo. Actualmente, la volatilidad vuelve a estar en niveles reducidos, lo que sugiere un mercado más comprimido y con menor amplitud de movimiento. La clave será ver si el próximo aumento del volumen agregado vuelve acompañado de una expansión de volatilidad. #Bitcoin by Carmelo Alemán, Analista On-Chain Santiment

BTC - Volatilidad 29/04/206. @santimentfeed Desde el pico de volatilidad (oscilación del precio en un tiempo determinado) registrados desde el 05 al 15 de febrero, Bitcoin ha entrado en una fase claramente distinta. La volatilidad de 1 semana se ha reducido de forma muy significativa, mientras el precio ha comenzado a moverse dentro de rangos de menor amplitud. Esto no significa ausencia de riesgo, pero sí refleja un cambio importante en la estructura del mercado: menos expansión violenta, menos desplazamientos agresivos y una fase de oscilación más comprimida. Cuando la volatilidad cae de forma tan marcada después de un episodio extremo, el mercado suele entrar en una etapa de espera. El precio se mueve, pero con menor magnitud relativa, mientras los participantes evalúan si el siguiente movimiento vendrá respaldado por demanda real o por una nueva expansión del apalancamiento. En este contexto, la clave no es solo observar el precio, sino entender la compresión de volatilidad. Bitcoin parece estar acumulando energía para su próximo movimiento relevante. #Bitcoin

Bitcoin Social Volume. .29/04/2026. @santimentfeed El Social Volume de Santiment mide menciones de Bitcoin en fuentes sociales cripto como X/Twitter, Telegram, Reddit, Bitcointalk, YouTube, 4chan /biz/ y Farcaster. No mide volumen negociado, sino conversación social. El volumen social de Bitcoin alcanzó su pico anual el 6 de febrero, con aproximadamente 8.732 menciones en el intervalo de 4 horas. Este repunte no apareció de forma aislada: llegó justo después de la fuerte liquidación del 5 de febrero, cuando el precio de BTC sufrió una caída agresiva. Desde entonces, el volumen social se ha reducido de forma considerable. Actualmente, Bitcoin sigue presente en redes, pero sin una explosión de conversación comparable a aquel episodio de estrés extremo. La importancia de esta métrica está en que los picos extremos de volumen social suelen funcionar como señal contraria, sobre todo si anteriormente no ha habido excesiva volatilidad. Cuando la conversación se dispara de forma agresiva, el precio suele moverse en sentido contrario, si el consenso social el grande, el precio oscila en el sentido contrario. En este caso, el pico del 6 de febrero reflejó un episodio de estrés social tras la liquidación previa del 5 de febrero. Desde entonces, la caída del Social Volume sugiere que Bitcoin ya no se encuentra bajo el mismo nivel de atención extrema. #Bitcoin

Photorealistic dystopian action Camera control is next level✨
